title: Thyroid Medications
subtitle: The nurse manages thyroid drugs — levothyroxine replaces low thyroid (take on an empty stomach, lifelong); antithyroid drugs lower an overactive thyroid.

patient.name: Thyroid Medications
patient.admitDate: The nurse safely manages thyroid medications.

nursesNote: Levothyroxine replaces thyroid hormone in hypothyroidism. The nurse teaches the patient to take it on an empty stomach in the morning at the same time daily, separate from calcium, iron, and antacids that reduce absorption, and to continue it lifelong without stopping; it takes weeks for full effect. Signs of too much (tachycardia, weight loss, nervousness, insomnia) should be reported, especially chest pain or palpitations, while too little causes fatigue, weight gain, and cold intolerance. Antithyroid drugs (methimazole, PTU) treat hyperthyroidism by lowering hormone production and also take weeks to work; the major risk is agranulocytosis, so the patient reports a sore throat or fever right away. The nurse monitors TSH and symptoms to guide dosing.
providerOrders: Teach levothyroxine on an empty stomach in the morning
Separate levothyroxine from calcium, iron, and antacids
Stress lifelong daily use; do not stop
Report signs of too much thyroid hormone (palpitations, chest pain)
On antithyroid drugs, report sore throat or fever (agranulocytosis)

steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.sub: What is true about thyroid medications?

opts.text: Levothyroxine treats hypothyroidism
Take levothyroxine on an empty stomach in the morning
Levothyroxine is usually lifelong
Levothyroxine works within hours
Antithyroid drugs treat hyperthyroidism
Tachycardia can signal too much levothyroxine
Separate levothyroxine from calcium and iron
Report palpitations or chest pain
Antithyroid drugs never affect blood counts
On antithyroid drugs, report sore throat/fever
Fatigue and cold suggest too little hormone
TSH is monitored to guide dosing

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: Order teaching a new levothyroxine patient

opts.text: Explain it treats low thyroid
Teach empty stomach, morning, same time
Separate from calcium, iron, antacids
Stress lifelong use; do not stop
Teach signs of too much to report
Used for hypothyroidism
Methimazole or PTU
Taken on an empty stomach in the morning
Report a sore throat for low WBC risk
Too much causes tachycardia and weight loss
Used for hyperthyroidism / Graves

steps.title: Take Action
steps.sub: A levothyroxine patient reports palpitations and weight loss

opts.text: Assess heart rate and symptoms
Recognize signs of too much hormone
Check how the patient takes the drug
Notify the provider for dose review
Document and monitor
'I take levothyroxine before breakfast'
'I'll stop it once I feel normal'
'I separate it from my calcium pill'
'Palpitations are nothing to report'
'On methimazole I'll report a sore throat'
'My thyroid pill should work the same day'
'I take it at the same time each day'
'I can double up if I miss several doses'
